title: Pork Tenderloin with Mashed Potatoes & Peas
description: This family-friendly meal features tender pork, creamy mashed potatoes, and sweet peas, all prepared with minimal fuss and only one or two pans. It's a comforting and nutritious dish that even the pickiest eaters will love.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat pork tenderloin dry with paper towels and season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear pork tenderloin for 2-3 minutes per side until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 15-20 minutes, or until internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C). Let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
While pork is roasting, peel and chop potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Place in a pot, cover with cold water, and bring to a boil. Cook for 15-20 minutes until fork-tender.
Drain potatoes and return to the pot. Add milk, butter, salt, and pepper. Mash until smooth and creamy.
In the last 5 minutes of potato cooking, add frozen peas to the boiling water to cook until tender-crisp.
Drain peas and gently stir into the mashed potatoes.
Slice the rested pork tenderloin into medallions. Serve immediately with mashed potatoes and peas.
tips: For extra flavor, add a sprig of fresh rosemary or thyme to the skillet while searing the pork.
If you prefer a smoother mash, use a potato ricer. For a chunkier texture, a hand masher works great.
Don't overcrowd the skillet when searing the pork; cook in batches if necessary to ensure a good sear.
